sections.paragraph:
(“borrowed,” [“flowing”], from the root זוּף), [Ziph], pr.name-

(1) of a town situate in the tribe of Judah, Jos 15:55 2Ch 11:8 in the neighbourhood of which was a desert of the same name, 1Sa 23:14, 15 1Sa 23:15. Hence the Gentile noun זַיפִי 1Sa 23:19 26:1 . [Now زيف Rob. ii. 191.]-(2) of a man, 1Ch 4:16.
